summary: PR #2710 converted ftCo_Shouldered.c from NonMatching to Matching, with the bot report showing ftCo_Shouldered_Anim reaching 100.00%. The patch removed TU-local named float/double constants from source and changed their symbol entries back to local @ labels, adjusted stack-padding/declaration shape, cleaned includes, restored a real ftColl_80078710 call, and corrected shouldered motion state field usage in ftCo_Shouldered_Anim.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source update for ftCo_Shouldered; includes cleanup, stack/declaration changes, and ftCo_Shouldered_Anim matching edits.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_Shouldered_Anim as new 100.00% match

key_files.role: Build configuration switched ftCo_Shouldered.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Symbol config changed three .sdata2 constants at 0x804D8750, 0x804D8754, and 0x804D8758 from global ftCo_ names to local @ labels.

what_changed.text: ftCo_Shouldered.c was linked as matching in configure.py.
The bot report credited one new match: main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Shouldered, item ftCo_Shouldered_Anim, +33 bytes, 92.98% to 100.00%.
Three source-level const definitions, ftCo_804D8750, ftCo_804D8754, and ftCo_804D8758, were removed from ftCo_Shouldered.c, and corresponding symbols were made local @ labels in symbols.txt.
Unused includes for ftCaptain/types.h and ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureCut.h were removed.

Stack/declaration shape changed: ftCo_8009C5A4 replaced a zeroed u8[8] local with u32 unused locals, ftCo_8009C640 gained PAD_STACK(8), and ftCo_Shouldered_Anim moved several locals to the top of the function before a PAD_STACK(16).
ftCo_Shouldered_Anim replaced a commented-out ftColl_80078710 call with an active call using gobj1, fp1->victim_gobj, and &fp1->dmg.facing_dir_1.
The final shouldered countdown logic now uses fp->mv.co.shouldered.x0 rather than fp->mv.ca.specials.grav.

smart_moves.text: Demoted generated-looking address-named constants back to local symbol labels instead of keeping source globals for ordinary 0, 1, and S32_TO_F32 constants.
Kept PAD_STACK use where the diff placed it after declarations, while leaving earlier stack shaping as u32 unused locals when a reviewer/commenter noted PAD_STACK placement limitations.
evidence_refs: review_comments: ThePlayerRolo asked whether u32 unused local should use PAD_STACK
review_comments: jellejurre replied that PAD_STACK can only be used at the end of the declarations list
raw_diff: PAD_STACK(8) in ftCo_8009C640 and PAD_STACK(16) in ftCo_Shouldered_Anim

decomp_lessons.lesson: For ftCommon matching work, do not keep generated address-named float/double constants in source when the patch evidence only supports local literal storage; symbol config can represent them as local @ labels instead.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed ftCo_804D8750 = 0, ftCo_804D8754 = 1, ftCo_804D8758 = S32_TO_F32
raw_diff: changed those .sdata2 symbols to local @219/@220/@237

decomp_lessons.lesson: Stack-shaping macros are sensitive to declaration placement; in this PR discussion, PAD_STACK was considered unsuitable for the early u32 unused locals because it could only be placed at the end of the declaration list.
evidence_refs: review_comments: 'shouldn't this use the PAD_STACK macro?'
review_comments: 'No, you van only do that at the end of the list of declarations'

decomp_lessons.lesson: When matching animation/damage code, replacing a placeholder/commented helper call with the recovered real call can be part of the final match, as shown by ftColl_80078710 in ftCo_Shouldered_Anim.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: commented ftColl_80078710(gobj) replaced with ftColl_80078710(gobj1, fp1->victim_gobj, &fp1->dmg.facing_dir_1)
text_corpus: bot report shows ftCo_Shouldered_Anim reached 100.00%

naming_conventions.name: ftCo_804D8750 / ftCo_804D8754 / ftCo_804D8758
naming_conventions.convention: Do not retain address-style global names for these ordinary local .sdata2 constants when PR evidence supports local @ labels.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt global ftCo_ names changed to @219/@220/@237

naming_conventions.name: fp, fp1, fp2, gobj1, hit, pos
naming_conventions.convention: The patch used short role/local names for Fighter pointers, Fighter_GObj pointers, HitCapsule, and Vec3 locals in ftCo_Shouldered_Anim.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Switched object status from NonMatching to Matching after source changes; bot report confirms ftCo_Shouldered_Anim reached 100.00%.
Used declaration-order and lifetime shaping: locals were hoisted to the start of ftCo_Shouldered_Anim, and explicit u32 unused locals replaced a zeroed u8 array in ftCo_8009C5A4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_Shouldered_Anim local declarations moved before Fighter* fp
raw_diff: ftCo_8009C5A4 u8 _[8] replaced by u32 unused1/unused2

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK in functions where placement fit the declaration layout, specifically PAD_STACK(8) in ftCo_8009C640 and PAD_STACK(16) in ftCo_Shouldered_Anim.
Corrected control/data flow in ftCo_Shouldered_Anim by removing an inner scope with redeclared fp1/fp2 and directly deriving fp2 from fp1->victim_gobj->user_data.

review_feedback.author: ThePlayerRolo
review_feedback.comment: Asked whether the new u32 unused local should use the PAD_STACK macro.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.comment: Replied that PAD_STACK could only be used at the end of the declaration list.

follow_up_queries: PAD_STACK end of declarations review comment
ftCommon shouldered x0 grav field correction

evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: changed file list and line counts
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for all three changed files
text_corpus: bot match report and review comments
notes: PR body was empty, so author rationale is inferred only from diff, bot report, and comments.
No full final source file beyond the diff was loaded; claims are limited to changed hunks.
The bot report supports matching improvement for ftCo_Shouldered_Anim, but no local build or objdiff was run by this intake agent.
The PAD_STACK placement statement is preserved as PR review discussion, not promoted as a universal accepted standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: ftCo_Shouldered matching used local .sdata2 labels instead of source-level address-named constants
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2710, ftCo_Shouldered.c removed source definitions for ftCo_804D8750, ftCo_804D8754, and ftCo_804D8758, while config/GALE01/symbols.txt changed those addresses to local @219, @220, and @237 labels. This is a concrete example of avoiding source globals for ordinary constants when only local literal storage is evidenced.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK placement was constrained by declaration order in ftCo_Shouldered
accepted_candidate_records.text: A reviewer asked whether explicit unused stack locals should use PAD_STACK; the author replied that PAD_STACK could only be used at the end of the declaration list. Keep this as PR-specific evidence for stack-shape tactics in this file rather than as a global rule without further corroboration.

source_update_candidates.title: ftCo_Shouldered.c matched in PR #2710
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2710 changed configure.py to mark melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Shouldered.c as Matching. The bot report listed ftCo_Shouldered_Anim as a new 100.00% match.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful path-scoped history for future ftCommon/shouldered matching work.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify PAD_STACK placement only if corroborated
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2710 contains a review exchange where PAD_STACK was not used for early unused locals because it could only be placed at the end of the declaration list. Consider adding an example or note only after curator verifies this is a broader project rule and not just a local matching constraint.
evidence_refs: review_comments: 'shouldn't this use the PAD_STACK macro?'
review_comments: 'No, you van only do that at the end of the list of declarations'
source_update_candidates.reason: The exchange is actionable but too narrow to promote automatically as a global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all u32 unused locals are preferable to PAD_STACK; this PR only documents a placement-specific discussion.
Do not claim the author intentionally fixed gameplay behavior; the evidence supports decomp matching and code-shape changes, not runtime intent.
Do not promote the local names fp1/fp2/gobj1 as semantic conventions beyond this function; they are only observed in the diff.
